Q1 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

If coagulant (Alum) dosage is fixed, then the best condition for coagulation process is:

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
• High Turbidity and High Alkalinity water is easy to treat as alum will react properly due to
proper alkalinity and flocs will form steadily.
• pH will be affected temporarily but treatment will be effective.
• High turbidity and low alkalinity water may need lime to improve alkalinity and optimize coagulation.
• Low turbidity and low alkalinity water is difficult to treat, requiring alkalinity correction
with lime and high doses of high molecular weight polyelectrolyte.

Q2 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

Which of the following is not a recognised limitation of the classical sag curve model used to predict dissolved oxygen levels in streams?

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)
• It is based on the assumption that there is one source of BOD when there may actually be several different point or non-point sources of BOD.
• Algal respiration in the absence of sunlight, nitrification processes that increase the oxygen demand and the presence of sludge deposits in pool areas can all increase a stream's BOD. It doesn't account for them.
• In shallow streams, masses of microbial growth attached to the streambed may consume more dissolved oxygen.
• It assumes steady-state conditions all along a river channel and does not incorporate varying
deoxygenation rate constants because of channel formation, obstacles, pools, etc.

Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0

An electrostatic precipitator is designed to remove particulate matter from a flue gas stream at a flow rate of 12 m3/s. The drift velocity of the charged particles is 0.12 m/s. If the required collection efficiency is 92%, then the minimum plate area (in m2) needed for the system is ______ . (Answer up to two decimal places).

NAT
Correct answer: 252.57
Solution

252.57 (252.45 - 252.70)

NAT
Correct answer: 18.83
Solution

Q5 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In the analysis of nitrogen content in sewage, which one of the following interpretations is most appropriate if only free ammonia and albuminoid nitrogen are detected in significant amounts, while nitrates are absent?

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)
• The free ammonia indicates the very first stage of decomposition of organic matter (thus indicating recently staled sewage).
• Albuminoid nitrogen indicates quantity of nitrogen present in sewage before the decomposition of organic matter has started.
• Nitrates indicate the most stable form of nitrogenous matter contained in sewage, thus indicating the well-oxidized and treated sewage.

Q6 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A 300 bottle is used for a BOD (Biochemical Oxygen Demand) test. 15 ml of waste water sample is pipetted directly into the incubation bottle. The initial dissolved oxygen in the diluted sample was 9.2 and after 5 days at 20°C, the dissolved oxygen dropped to 1.8 A blank containing only dilution water showed an initial dissolved oxygen of 9 and final dissolved oxygen of 8.7 . The 5-day BOD of the waste water sample (in ) is _____.
(Round off upto nearest integer)

NAT
Correct answer: 142
Solution

142 (141 - 143)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • CIVIL ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Which of the following statements about oxidation/stabilization ponds is/are correct?

Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(a, b)
• In a facultative pond, the upper layers work under aerobic conditions, while the anaerobic
conditions prevail in the bottom layer.
• The totally aerobic ponds will have to be of very small depths and still then may require
occasional stirring, to prevent anaerobic conditions in the settled sludge.
• The production of algae and oxidation of organic matter result in a net reduction in BOD,
since algae are more stable than the original organic content.
• The Effluents from oxidation ponds can be easily used for land irrigation, especially in places
where direct discharge into rivers is not feasible.

The quantity of waste sludge withdrawn daily is 240 m3. Based on the above data, which of the following values are incorrect:

Correct answer: 69.44
Solution

69.44 (68 - 72)
